---
---
title: "Comprehensive Oura Sleep Analysis Report"
author: "Rishi Siddharth"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 12
    fig-height: 8
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: python3
---
```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from pathlib import Path
from scipy import stats
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression, Ridge
from statsmodels.nonparametric.smoothers_lowess import lowess
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import STL
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

# Set up plotting style
plt.style.use('default')
sns.set_palette("husl")
sns.set_context("notebook", font_scale=1.1)

# Load data
data_path = Path("/Users/rishi/Desktop/OURA/oura_daily_clean.csv")
df = pd.read_csv(data_path)
df['day'] = pd.to_datetime(df['day'])

# Calculate sleep stage percentages if not present
if 'deep_sleep_pct' not in df.columns:
    df['deep_sleep_pct'] = (df['deep_sleep_mins'] / df['total_sleep_mins']) * 100
    df['light_sleep_pct'] = (df['light_sleep_mins'] / df['total_sleep_mins']) * 100
    df['rem_sleep_pct'] = (df['rem_sleep_mins'] / df['total_sleep_mins']) * 100

# Add weekday features
df['weekday'] = df['day'].dt.day_name()
df['weekday_num'] = df['day'].dt.dayofweek
df['is_weekend'] = df['weekday_num'].isin([5, 6])

# ACL event window setup
ACL_START = pd.Timestamp("2025-06-01")
ACL_END = pd.Timestamp("2025-07-15")
df['is_acl_window'] = (df['day'] >= ACL_START) & (df['day'] <= ACL_END)

# ACL phases
df['acl_phase'] = 'other'
df.loc[(df['day'] >= ACL_START - pd.Timedelta(days=30)) & (df['day'] < ACL_START), 'acl_phase'] = 'pre'
df.loc[df['is_acl_window'], 'acl_phase'] = 'during'
df.loc[(df['day'] > ACL_END) & (df['day'] <= ACL_END + pd.Timedelta(days=30)), 'acl_phase'] = 'post'

# Create lag features for activity metrics
for col in ['steps', 'active_calories']:
    df[f'{col}_lag0'] = df[col]
    df[f'{col}_lag1'] = df[col].shift(1)
    df[f'{col}_lag2'] = df[col].shift(2)

print(f"Dataset loaded: {df.shape[0]} days from {df['day'].min().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} to {df['day'].max().strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}")
```

# Exploratory Data Analysis - Oura Dataset

This notebook provides an initial exploration of the Oura daily dataset, including data quality checks, basic statistics, and preliminary visualizations.

## Data Overview
```{python}
#| label: data-overview

print(f"Dataset shape: {df.shape}")
print(f"Date range: {df['day'].min()} to {df['day'].max()}")
print(f"Total days: {len(df)}")
```

## Data Quality Assessment
```{python}
#| label: data-quality

print("=== DATA QUALITY ASSESSMENT ===")
print("\nMissing values by column:")
missing_data = df.isnull().sum()
missing_pct = (missing_data / len(df)) * 100
missing_df = pd.DataFrame({
    'Missing Count': missing_data,
    'Missing %': missing_pct
}).sort_values('Missing Count', ascending=False)

display(missing_df[missing_df['Missing Count'] > 0])

print(f"\nTotal missing values: {df.isnull().sum().sum()}")
print(f"Complete cases: {df.dropna().shape[0]} / {len(df)} ({df.dropna().shape[0]/len(df)*100:.1f}%)")
```

## Core Metrics Summary
```{python}
#| label: core-metrics

print("=== CORE METRICS SUMMARY ===")
core_metrics = ['sleep_score', 'readiness_score', 'activity_score', 
                'total_sleep_mins', 'steps', 'average_hrv']

summary_stats = df[core_metrics].describe()
display(summary_stats.round(2))
```

## Sleep Stage Analysis
```{python}
#| label: sleep-stages

print("=== SLEEP STAGE ANALYSIS ===")
sleep_stages = ['deep_sleep_mins', 'light_sleep_mins', 'rem_sleep_mins', 'awake_mins']
stage_summary = df[sleep_stages + ['total_sleep_mins']].describe()
display(stage_summary.round(2))

print("\nSleep stage percentages:")
stage_pct_cols = ['deep_sleep_pct', 'light_sleep_pct', 'rem_sleep_pct']
stage_pct_summary = df[stage_pct_cols].describe()
display(stage_pct_summary.round(2))
```

## Time Series Overview
```{python}
#| label: time-series-overview
#| fig-cap: "Core metrics tracked over time"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

# Sleep score over time
axes[0, 0].plot(df['day'], df['sleep_score'], 'b-', alpha=0.7)
axes[0, 0].set_title('Sleep Score Over Time')
axes[0, 0].set_ylabel('Sleep Score')
axes[0, 0].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

# Total sleep over time
axes[0, 1].plot(df['day'], df['total_sleep_mins'], 'g-', alpha=0.7)
axes[0, 1].set_title('Total Sleep Duration Over Time')
axes[0, 1].set_ylabel('Sleep Duration (minutes)')
axes[0, 1].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

# Steps over time
axes[1, 0].plot(df['day'], df['steps'], 'r-', alpha=0.7)
axes[1, 0].set_title('Daily Steps Over Time')
axes[1, 0].set_ylabel('Steps')
axes[1, 0].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

# HRV over time
axes[1, 1].plot(df['day'], df['average_hrv'], 'm-', alpha=0.7)
axes[1, 1].set_title('Average HRV Over Time')
axes[1, 1].set_ylabel('HRV (ms)')
axes[1, 1].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

This is interesting. Although heart rate variability and total sleep are pretty similar over time, I have not walked as much since returning to university in DC. In London at LSE I was walking much more, and reached over 20,000 steps a day a fair amount. Also, for heartrate variability there was a huge spike last month. This might have been a glitch with the data or according to google "135 ms is a high value, but its interpretation depends entirely on your individual baseline and other personal factors like age and fitness level."

## Correlation Matrix
```{python}
#| label: correlation-matrix
#| fig-cap: "Correlation matrix of core metrics"

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
correlation_matrix = df[core_metrics].corr()
sns.heatmap(correlation_matrix, annot=True, cmap='coolwarm', center=0, 
            square=True, fmt='.2f')
plt.title('Correlation Matrix of Core Metrics')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## ACL Event Window Analysis
```{python}
#| label: acl-analysis

print("=== ACL EVENT WINDOW ANALYSIS ===")
acl_phases = df['acl_phase'].value_counts()
print("ACL Phase distribution:")
print(acl_phases)

print(f"\nACL window dates: {ACL_START} to {ACL_END}")
print(f"Days in ACL window: {df['is_acl_window'].sum()}")

# Check data availability around ACL event
pre_data = df[df['acl_phase'] == 'pre']
during_data = df[df['acl_phase'] == 'during']
post_data = df[df['acl_phase'] == 'post']

print(f"\nData availability:")
print(f"Pre-ACL (30 days before): {len(pre_data)} days")
print(f"During ACL: {len(during_data)} days")
print(f"Post-ACL (30 days after): {len(post_data)} days")
```

# Sleep Stages vs Sleep Score Analysis

This notebook analyzes the relationship between sleep stage percentages and sleep score, including correlation analysis and predictive modeling.

## Sleep Stage Composition
```{python}
#| label: stage-composition

# Calculate average sleep stage composition
avg_composition = pd.DataFrame({
    'Stage': ['REM', 'Deep', 'Light'],
    'Percentage': [df['rem_sleep_pct'].mean(), 
                   df['deep_sleep_pct'].mean(),
                   df['light_sleep_pct'].mean()]
})

fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 5))

# Pie chart
colors = ['#ff9999', '#66b3ff', '#99ff99']
ax1.pie(avg_composition['Percentage'], labels=avg_composition['Stage'], 
        colors=colors, autopct='%1.1f%%', startangle=90)
ax1.set_title('Average Sleep Stage Composition')

# Distribution plots
ax2.hist(df['rem_sleep_pct'].dropna(), bins=30, alpha=0.7, label='REM %', color='#ff9999')
ax2.hist(df['deep_sleep_pct'].dropna(), bins=30, alpha=0.7, label='Deep %', color='#66b3ff')
ax2.hist(df['light_sleep_pct'].dropna(), bins=30, alpha=0.7, label='Light %', color='#99ff99')
ax2.set_xlabel('Percentage')
ax2.set_ylabel('Frequency')
ax2.set_title('Sleep Stage Percentage Distributions')
ax2.legend()

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"Average REM%: {df['rem_sleep_pct'].mean():.1f}%")
print(f"Average Deep%: {df['deep_sleep_pct'].mean():.1f}%")
print(f"Average Light%: {df['light_sleep_pct'].mean():.1f}%")
```

Light sleep dominates the majority of sleep, while REM (Rapid Eye Movement), the stage in which we, "which is a stage of sleep characterized by rapid eye movements behind the eyelids, active brain activity, and vivid dreams. It is one of two main types of sleep, the other being non-REM sleep. During REM sleep, the brain is almost as active as when you're awake, but your body experiences a temporary muscle paralysis". Deep sleep is similar at 21.73%, where we have physical restoration, tissue repair, immune system function, growth hormone release, and memory consolidation in this stage. It occurs in the first few hours of sleep, after stage 3 (deep sleep) and before REM sleep. The rest is made up of light sleep.

## Correlation Analysis
```{python}
#| label: correlation-analysis

print("=== CORRELATION ANALYSIS ===")

# Calculate correlations
def calculate_correlations(df, x_vars, y_var, method='pearson'):
    results = []
    for x in x_vars:
        if method == 'pearson':
            corr = df[x].corr(df[y_var])
        else:
            corr = df[x].corr(df[y_var], method='spearman')
        results.append({'variable': x, 'correlation': corr})
    return pd.DataFrame(results)

# Pearson correlations
pearson_corrs = calculate_correlations(df, stage_pct_cols, 'sleep_score', method='pearson')
print("Pearson correlations with sleep score:")
display(pearson_corrs.round(3))

# Spearman correlations
spearman_corrs = calculate_correlations(df, stage_pct_cols, 'sleep_score', method='spearman')
print("\nSpearman correlations with sleep score:")
display(spearman_corrs.round(3))

# Partial correlations controlling for total sleep
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression

def calculate_partial_correlation(df, x, y, control):
    # Remove NaN values
    clean_df = df[[x, y, control]].dropna()
    
    # Residualize x
    X_control = clean_df[[control]]
    x_model = LinearRegression().fit(X_control, clean_df[x])
    x_resid = clean_df[x] - x_model.predict(X_control)
    
    # Residualize y
    y_model = LinearRegression().fit(X_control, clean_df[y])
    y_resid = clean_df[y] - y_model.predict(X_control)
    
    # Correlation of residuals
    return np.corrcoef(x_resid, y_resid)[0, 1]

partial_corrs = []
for col in stage_pct_cols:
    partial_r = calculate_partial_correlation(df, col, 'sleep_score', 'total_sleep_mins')
    partial_corrs.append({'variable': col, 'partial_correlation': partial_r})

partial_corrs_df = pd.DataFrame(partial_corrs)
print("\nPartial correlations (controlling for total sleep duration):")
display(partial_corrs_df.round(3))
```

Wow. So based on this data and the pearson correlation "a statistical measure that quantifies the linear relationship between two variables, ranging from -1 to +1." This shows that a **higher sleep scores are most strongly correlated with higher REM sleep percentage (r ≈ 0.57)**. After controlling for total sleep duration, REM and deep sleep both predict higher sleep quality, while light sleep percentage is negatively associated, suggesting that stage balance — not just total sleep time — is key to better sleep outcomes.

## Model Fitting
```{python}
#| label: model-fitting

print("=== MODEL FITTING ===")

# Prepare data for modeling
model_vars = stage_pct_cols + ['total_sleep_mins']
model_df = df[model_vars + ['sleep_score']].dropna()
X = model_df[model_vars]
y = model_df['sleep_score']

# OLS model
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
from sklearn.metrics import r2_score

ols_model = LinearRegression()
ols_model.fit(X, y)
ols_pred = ols_model.predict(X)
ols_r2 = r2_score(y, ols_pred)

ols_coefficients = pd.DataFrame({
    'variable': model_vars,
    'coefficient': ols_model.coef_,
    'abs_coefficient': np.abs(ols_model.coef_)
}).sort_values('abs_coefficient', ascending=False)

print("OLS Model Results:")
print(f"R-squared: {ols_r2:.3f}")
print(f"Number of observations: {len(model_df)}")
print("\nCoefficients:")
display(ols_coefficients[['variable', 'coefficient']].round(3))

# Ridge regression
from sklearn.linear_model import Ridge

ridge_model = Ridge(alpha=1.0)
ridge_model.fit(X, y)
ridge_pred = ridge_model.predict(X)
ridge_r2 = r2_score(y, ridge_pred)

ridge_coefficients = pd.DataFrame({
    'variable': model_vars,
    'coefficient': ridge_model.coef_,
    'abs_coefficient': np.abs(ridge_model.coef_)
}).sort_values('abs_coefficient', ascending=False)

print(f"\nRidge Model Results:")
print(f"R-squared: {ridge_r2:.3f}")
print(f"Number of observations: {len(model_df)}")
print("\nCoefficients:")
display(ridge_coefficients[['variable', 'coefficient']].round(3))
```

The model explains about 81% of what drives my sleep score. REM % and total sleep minutes are the strongest independent predictors, with deep sleep helping modestly and light sleep hurting when controlling for the rest. The high R² reflects the combined predictive power of these factors, not just one strong correlation.

## Sleep Stages vs Sleep Score Visualizations
```{python}
#| label: stage-scatter-plots
#| fig-cap: "Relationship between sleep stages and sleep score with LOWESS smoothing"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))

# Define function for scatter with LOWESS
def plot_scatter_with_lowess(x_data, y_data, title, ax):
    # Remove NaN values
    mask = ~(x_data.isna() | y_data.isna())
    x_clean = x_data[mask]
    y_clean = y_data[mask]
    
    # Scatter plot
    ax.scatter(x_clean, y_clean, alpha=0.5, s=30)
    
    # Add LOWESS smoothing
    if len(x_clean) > 10:
        lowess_result = lowess(y_clean, x_clean, frac=0.3)
        ax.plot(lowess_result[:, 0], lowess_result[:, 1], 'r-', linewidth=2, label='LOWESS')
    
    # Add linear regression line
    z = np.polyfit(x_clean, y_clean, 1)
    p = np.poly1d(z)
    ax.plot(sorted(x_clean), p(sorted(x_clean)), 'g--', alpha=0.7, label='Linear')
    
    ax.set_xlabel(title.split(' vs ')[0])
    ax.set_ylabel('Sleep Score')
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.legend()
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

# REM% vs Sleep Score
plot_scatter_with_lowess(df['rem_sleep_pct'], df['sleep_score'], 
                         'REM% vs Sleep Score', axes[0])

# Deep% vs Sleep Score
plot_scatter_with_lowess(df['deep_sleep_pct'], df['sleep_score'], 
                         'Deep% vs Sleep Score', axes[1])

# Light% vs Sleep Score
plot_scatter_with_lowess(df['light_sleep_pct'], df['sleep_score'], 
                         'Light% vs Sleep Score', axes[2])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

These graphs show that sleep quality improves with a balanced distribution of stages (high REM (20–25%)), moderate deep (15–20%), and controlled light (<55%). The strong nonlinear rise in REM and sharp drop in light sleep indicate that stage balance matters more than total hours. The model's R² = 0.81 reflects how these three proportions, combined with total duration, jointly capture over 80% of what drives sleep score variation.

## Coefficient Plot
```{python}
#| label: coefficient-plot
#| fig-cap: "OLS model coefficients for sleep score prediction"

if ols_coefficients.shape[0] > 0:
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    
    # Sort by coefficient value
    coef_plot_df = ols_coefficients.sort_values('coefficient')
    
    # Create horizontal bar plot
    colors = ['red' if x < 0 else 'green' for x in coef_plot_df['coefficient']]
    plt.barh(range(len(coef_plot_df)), coef_plot_df['coefficient'], color=colors, alpha=0.7)
    plt.yticks(range(len(coef_plot_df)), coef_plot_df['variable'])
    plt.xlabel('Coefficient Value')
    plt.title('OLS Model Coefficients (Sleep Score Prediction)')
    plt.axvline(x=0, color='black', linestyle='-', linewidth=0.5)
    plt.grid(axis='x', alpha=0.3)
    
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
```

# Weekday vs Weekend Sleep Duration Trends

This notebook analyzes sleep patterns by day of the week and weekend vs weekday differences, including seasonal decomposition.

## Weekday Distribution
```{python}
#| label: weekday-distribution

print("=== WEEKDAY DISTRIBUTION ===")
weekday_counts = df['weekday'].value_counts()
display(weekday_counts)

print(f"\nWeekend vs Weekday distribution:")
print(f"Weekend days: {df['is_weekend'].sum()}")
print(f"Weekday days: {(~df['is_weekend']).sum()}")
```

## Sleep Duration by Weekday
```{python}
#| label: sleep-by-weekday

print("=== SLEEP DURATION BY WEEKDAY ===")
weekday_order = ['Monday', 'Tuesday', 'Wednesday', 'Thursday', 'Friday', 'Saturday', 'Sunday']
weekday_sleep = df.groupby('weekday')['total_sleep_mins'].agg(['mean', 'std', 'count']).reindex(weekday_order).round(2)
display(weekday_sleep)

# Weekend vs weekday comparison
print("\n=== WEEKEND VS WEEKDAY COMPARISON ===")
weekend_sleep = df[df['is_weekend']]['total_sleep_mins']
weekday_sleep_vals = df[~df['is_weekend']]['total_sleep_mins']

print(f"Weekend sleep duration:")
print(f"  Mean: {weekend_sleep.mean():.1f} minutes")
print(f"  Std: {weekend_sleep.std():.1f} minutes")
print(f"  Count: {len(weekend_sleep)} days")

print(f"\nWeekday sleep duration:")
print(f"  Mean: {weekday_sleep_vals.mean():.1f} minutes")
print(f"  Std: {weekday_sleep_vals.std():.1f} minutes")
print(f"  Count: {len(weekday_sleep_vals)} days")

# Statistical test
t_stat, p_value = stats.ttest_ind(weekend_sleep, weekday_sleep_vals)
print(f"\nT-test results:")
print(f"  T-statistic: {t_stat:.3f}")
print(f"  P-value: {p_value:.3f}")
print(f"  Difference: {weekend_sleep.mean() - weekday_sleep_vals.mean():.1f} minutes")
```

## Sleep Duration Visualizations
```{python}
#| label: weekday-visualizations
#| fig-cap: "Sleep duration patterns by day of week"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

# Box plot by weekday
weekday_data = []
for day in weekday_order:
    day_data = df[df['weekday'] == day]['total_sleep_mins'].dropna()
    weekday_data.append(day_data)

bp = axes[0].boxplot(weekday_data, labels=[d[:3] for d in weekday_order], patch_artist=True)
for i, box in enumerate(bp['boxes']):
    if i >= 5:  # Weekend
        box.set_facecolor('lightcoral')
    else:  # Weekday
        box.set_facecolor('lightblue')

axes[0].set_ylabel('Sleep Duration (minutes)')
axes[0].set_title('Sleep Duration by Weekday')
axes[0].axhline(480, color='green', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='8 hours')
axes[0].legend()
axes[0].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

# Weekend vs weekday comparison with confidence intervals
def calculate_confidence_interval(data, confidence=0.95):
    n = len(data)
    mean = data.mean()
    se = stats.sem(data.dropna())
    margin = se * stats.t.ppf((1 + confidence) / 2, n - 1)
    return mean - margin, mean + margin

weekend_ci = calculate_confidence_interval(weekend_sleep)
weekday_ci = calculate_confidence_interval(weekday_sleep_vals)

comparison_data = pd.DataFrame({
    'group': ['Weekday', 'Weekend'],
    'mean': [weekday_sleep_vals.mean(), weekend_sleep.mean()],
    'ci_low': [weekday_ci[0], weekend_ci[0]],
    'ci_high': [weekday_ci[1], weekend_ci[1]]
})

x_pos = [0, 1]
axes[1].bar(x_pos, comparison_data['mean'], color=['lightblue', 'lightcoral'], alpha=0.7)
axes[1].errorbar(x_pos, comparison_data['mean'], 
                 yerr=[comparison_data['mean'] - comparison_data['ci_low'],
                       comparison_data['ci_high'] - comparison_data['mean']],
                 fmt='none', color='black', capsize=5)

axes[1].set_xticks(x_pos)
axes[1].set_xticklabels(comparison_data['group'])
axes[1].set_ylabel('Sleep Duration (minutes)')
axes[1].set_title('Weekend vs Weekday Sleep Duration (95% CI)')
axes[1].axhline(480, color='green', linestyle='--', alpha=0.5, label='8 hours')
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## STL Decomposition
```{python}
#| label: stl-decomposition
#| fig-cap: "STL decomposition of total sleep duration"

print("Testing STL decomposition...")

# Prepare time series data
df_ts = df.set_index('day').sort_index()
sleep_series = df_ts['total_sleep_mins'].dropna()

# Perform STL decomposition
stl = STL(sleep_series, period=7, seasonal=13)
stl_result = stl.fit()

print("✅ STL decomposition completed!")
print(f"Trend range: {stl_result.trend.min():.1f} - {stl_result.trend.max():.1f}")
print(f"Seasonal range: {stl_result.seasonal.min():.1f} - {stl_result.seasonal.max():.1f}")

# Plot STL decomposition
fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(14, 10))

# Original series
axes[0].plot(sleep_series.index, sleep_series.values, 'b-', alpha=0.7)
axes[0].set_ylabel('Original')
axes[0].set_title('STL Decomposition of Total Sleep Duration')
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Trend
axes[1].plot(stl_result.trend.index, stl_result.trend.values, 'g-', linewidth=2)
axes[1].set_ylabel('Trend')
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Seasonal
axes[2].plot(stl_result.seasonal.index, stl_result.seasonal.values, 'r-')
axes[2].set_ylabel('Seasonal')
axes[2].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Residual
axes[3].plot(stl_result.resid.index, stl_result.resid.values, 'm-', alpha=0.7)
axes[3].set_ylabel('Residual')
axes[3].set_xlabel('Date')
axes[3].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

for ax in axes[:-1]:
    ax.set_xticklabels([])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

The sleep duration follows a long-term rhythm: **shorter average sleep toward late 2024, peaking mid-2025, then declining again**. This is overlaid with strong short-term variability that suggests inconsistent nightly habits. I have what can be seen as cyclical "recovery" spikes but no fixed weekly periodicity. The STL results confirm my total sleep behavior is driven by mid-term lifestyle trends more than strict day-to-day regularity.

# Activity to Sleep Lags & ACL Event Study

This notebook analyzes the relationship between daily activity and sleep, including lag effects, and performs an event study around the ACL injury.

## Lag Features Summary
```{python}
#| label: lag-features

# Check lag features
lag_cols = ['steps_lag0', 'steps_lag1', 'steps_lag2', 
           'active_calories_lag0', 'active_calories_lag1', 'active_calories_lag2']

print("=== LAG FEATURES SUMMARY ===")
print("Lag features availability:")
for col in lag_cols:
    non_null_count = df[col].notna().sum()
    print(f"  {col}: {non_null_count} observations")

print(f"\nTotal observations: {len(df)}")
print(f"Date range: {df['day'].min()} to {df['day'].max()}")
```

## Activity-Sleep Correlations
```{python}
#| label: activity-correlations

print("=== ACTIVITY-SLEEP CORRELATIONS ===")

# Same-night correlations
same_night_corrs = []
for activity in ['steps', 'active_calories']:
    corr = df[activity].corr(df['total_sleep_mins'])
    same_night_corrs.append({'activity': activity, 'lag': 'same_night', 'correlation': corr})

# Next-night correlations
next_night_corrs = []
for activity in ['steps', 'active_calories']:
    lag_col = f"{activity}_lag1"
    corr = df[lag_col].corr(df['total_sleep_mins'])
    next_night_corrs.append({'activity': activity, 'lag': 'next_night', 'correlation': corr})

corr_df = pd.DataFrame(same_night_corrs + next_night_corrs)
display(corr_df.round(3))
```

## Lag Model Fitting
```{python}
#| label: lag-models

print("=== LAG MODEL FITTING ===")

# Fit models with different lag structures
lag_models = {}

# Steps model
steps_vars = ['steps_lag0', 'steps_lag1', 'steps_lag2']
steps_df = df[steps_vars + ['total_sleep_mins']].dropna()
if len(steps_df) > 0:
    X_steps = steps_df[steps_vars]
    y_steps = steps_df['total_sleep_mins']
    
    steps_model = LinearRegression()
    steps_model.fit(X_steps, y_steps)
    steps_r2 = steps_model.score(X_steps, y_steps)
    
    lag_models['steps'] = {
        'r2': steps_r2,
        'n_obs': len(steps_df),
        'coefficients': pd.DataFrame({
            'variable': steps_vars,
            'coefficient': steps_model.coef_
        })
    }

# Calories model
cal_vars = ['active_calories_lag0', 'active_calories_lag1', 'active_calories_lag2']
cal_df = df[cal_vars + ['total_sleep_mins']].dropna()
if len(cal_df) > 0:
    X_cal = cal_df[cal_vars]
    y_cal = cal_df['total_sleep_mins']
    
    cal_model = LinearRegression()
    cal_model.fit(X_cal, y_cal)
    cal_r2 = cal_model.score(X_cal, y_cal)
    
    lag_models['active_calories'] = {
        'r2': cal_r2,
        'n_obs': len(cal_df),
        'coefficients': pd.DataFrame({
            'variable': cal_vars,
            'coefficient': cal_model.coef_
        })
    }

for model_name, results in lag_models.items():
    print(f"\n{model_name.upper()} Model:")
    print(f"  R-squared: {results['r2']:.3f}")
    print(f"  Observations: {results['n_obs']}")
    print("  Coefficients:")
    display(results['coefficients'].round(3))
```

## Activity vs Sleep Scatter Plots
```{python}
#| label: activity-scatter
#| fig-cap: "Relationship between activity and sleep with same-night and next-night effects"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

def plot_activity_sleep(x_data, y_data, title, ax):
    mask = ~(x_data.isna() | y_data.isna())
    x_clean = x_data[mask]
    y_clean = y_data[mask]
    
    if len(x_clean) > 0:
        ax.scatter(x_clean, y_clean, alpha=0.5, s=30)
        
        if len(x_clean) > 10:
            lowess_result = lowess(y_clean, x_clean, frac=0.3)
            ax.plot(lowess_result[:, 0], lowess_result[:, 1], 'r-', linewidth=2, label='LOWESS')
        
        z = np.polyfit(x_clean, y_clean, 1)
        p = np.poly1d(z)
        ax.plot(sorted(x_clean), p(sorted(x_clean)), 'g--', alpha=0.7, label='Linear')
        
        ax.legend()
    
    ax.set_xlabel(title.split(' vs ')[0])
    ax.set_ylabel('Total Sleep (mins)')
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

# Same-night effects
plot_activity_sleep(df['steps'], df['total_sleep_mins'], 
                   'Steps vs Sleep (Same Night)', axes[0, 0])
plot_activity_sleep(df['active_calories'], df['total_sleep_mins'], 
                   'Active Calories vs Sleep (Same Night)', axes[0, 1])

# Next-night effects
plot_activity_sleep(df['steps_lag1'], df['total_sleep_mins'], 
                   'Steps vs Sleep (Next Night)', axes[1, 0])
plot_activity_sleep(df['active_calories_lag1'], df['total_sleep_mins'], 
                   'Active Calories vs Sleep (Next Night)', axes[1, 1])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## ACL Event Study
```{python}
#| label: acl-event-study

print("=== ACL EVENT STUDY ===")

# Create event slices
event_slices_data = {
    'pre': df[df['acl_phase'] == 'pre'],
    'during': df[df['acl_phase'] == 'during'],
    'post': df[df['acl_phase'] == 'post']
}

print("Event study periods:")
for phase, phase_df in event_slices_data.items():
    print(f"  {phase}: {len(phase_df)} days")
    if len(phase_df) > 0:
        print(f"    Date range: {phase_df['day'].min()} to {phase_df['day'].max()}")

# Calculate statistics for each phase
acl_metrics = ['sleep_score', 'total_sleep_mins', 'average_hrv', 'readiness_score', 'steps']
comparison_stats = []

for phase, phase_df in event_slices_data.items():
    if len(phase_df) > 0:
        for metric in acl_metrics:
            if metric in phase_df.columns:
                mean_val = phase_df[metric].mean()
                se = stats.sem(phase_df[metric].dropna())
                ci_margin = se * stats.t.ppf(0.975, len(phase_df) - 1)
                comparison_stats.append({
                    'phase': phase,
                    'metric': metric,
                    'mean': mean_val,
                    'ci_low': mean_val - ci_margin,
                    'ci_high': mean_val + ci_margin,
                    'n_obs': len(phase_df)
                })

comparison_df = pd.DataFrame(comparison_stats)
print("\nPhase comparison statistics:")
display(comparison_df.round(2))
```

## ACL Timeline Visualization
```{python}
#| label: acl-timeline
#| fig-cap: "Key metrics timeline with ACL injury window highlighted"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

def plot_timeline_with_window(data, x_col, y_col, start_date, end_date, title, ax):
    ax.plot(data[x_col], data[y_col], 'b-', alpha=0.7)
    
    # Highlight ACL window
    ax.axvspan(start_date, end_date, alpha=0.2, color='red', label='ACL Window')
    
    # Add rolling average
    data_sorted = data.sort_values(x_col)
    rolling_avg = data_sorted[y_col].rolling(window=7, min_periods=1).mean()
    ax.plot(data_sorted[x_col], rolling_avg, 'g-', linewidth=2, alpha=0.8, label='7-day avg')
    
    ax.set_xlabel('Date')
    ax.set_ylabel(y_col.replace('_', ' ').title())
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.legend()
    ax.tick_params(axis='x', rotation=45)
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Sleep score timeline
plot_timeline_with_window(df, 'day', 'sleep_score', ACL_START, ACL_END,
                          'Sleep Score Timeline with ACL Window', axes[0, 0])

# Total sleep timeline
plot_timeline_with_window(df, 'day', 'total_sleep_mins', ACL_START, ACL_END,
                          'Total Sleep Duration Timeline with ACL Window', axes[0, 1])

# HRV timeline
plot_timeline_with_window(df, 'day', 'average_hrv', ACL_START, ACL_END,
                          'Average HRV Timeline with ACL Window', axes[1, 0])

# Steps timeline
plot_timeline_with_window(df, 'day', 'steps', ACL_START, ACL_END,
                          'Daily Steps Timeline with ACL Window', axes[1, 1])

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Pre/During/Post ACL Comparison
```{python}
#| label: acl-comparison
#| fig-cap: "Comparison of key metrics across ACL injury phases"

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

metrics_to_plot = ['sleep_score', 'total_sleep_mins', 'average_hrv', 'steps']

for i, metric in enumerate(metrics_to_plot):
    row, col = i // 2, i % 2
    
    # Filter data for this metric
    metric_data = comparison_df[comparison_df['metric'] == metric]
    
    if len(metric_data) > 0:
        phases = ['pre', 'during', 'post']
        phase_labels = ['Pre-ACL', 'During ACL', 'Post-ACL']
        colors = ['blue', 'red', 'green']
        
        means = []
        errors = []
        valid_phases = []
        valid_colors = []
        
        for j, phase in enumerate(phases):
            phase_data = metric_data[metric_data['phase'] == phase]
            if len(phase_data) > 0:
                mean_val = phase_data['mean'].iloc[0]
                ci_low = phase_data['ci_low'].iloc[0]
                ci_high = phase_data['ci_high'].iloc[0]
                
                means.append(mean_val)
                errors.append([mean_val - ci_low, ci_high - mean_val])
                valid_phases.append(phase_labels[j])
                valid_colors.append(colors[j])
        
        if means:
            x_pos = range(len(means))
            bars = axes[row, col].bar(x_pos, means, color=valid_colors, alpha=0.7)
            
            # Add error bars
            errors_array = np.array(errors).T
            axes[row, col].errorbar(x_pos, means, yerr=errors_array, 
                                   fmt='none', color='black', capsize=5)
            
            axes[row, col].set_xticks(x_pos)
            axes[row, col].set_xticklabels(valid_phases)
            axes[row, col].set_ylabel(metric.replace('_', ' ').title())
            axes[row, col].set_title(f'{metric.replace("_", " ").title()} by ACL Phase')
            axes[row, col].grid(axis='y', alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Summary

This comprehensive analysis reveals key insights about sleep patterns, stage composition, and the impact of activity on sleep quality. The ACL injury event study shows measurable changes in sleep and recovery metrics during the injury period, with notable recovery patterns in the post-injury phase.
